text: You guys straight up suck! Service isn't like when it was sprint alone. My service is no better! And u ppl aren't even listening to us customers!

I had t mobile since 2017 and the t in T mobile has to stand for THEFT 
I hate that they charge for every damn thing!! 
Hidden charges too! Their service and reception sucks. You pay them all this money to keep a phone on for suck service! EFF T MOBILe
Ever-sense t mobile teamed up with sprint there has no change in the service it trash asf

I give t-mobile 5 stars because through this whole thing,  they have been the most understanding and compassionate business I've encountered!! Without a doubt you can truly see they really care genuinely about their  customers. My bill is high because I've been out of employment a few months but they have stood by my side. As long as I call and keep them informed they are willing to help do whatever they can to keep you running! And now I found a new job,  my bills will be paid up but if it wasn't  for them  helping me keep my phone on, I would of  never found it! T-mobile  your kindness and compassion during this pandemic  will NEVER EVER be forgotten!! Thank you!

Angel

T mobile is going down hill. The Agents just messed me all around with my phone bill. Poor customer service and my phone service is getting horrible.  I will not recommend and looking for new service elsewhere

I had a trac phone never lost a call or text trouble . I wanted unlimited data so I went to T Mobile.  I am starting 2nd month calls constantly  dropped text never  go through.  Data rarely works . I did call T Mobile they reset some setting worse than ever .Luckily  I am not in a contract . I  guess back to my track phone it never let me down .
Super overpriced service!

From day one I've paid for 360 protection on our phones because I have teenage boys and accidents happen. I've had them 1 yr and 2 mos and just tried to use it for the first time. You get the run around when try to file a claim. Online wouldn't work, on the phone wouldn't work, went to the store, they gave me a 1-800 number to call. The 1-800 number tells you to go the website or puts you on perpetual hold. We finally after a week got ahold of someone and filed a claim it cost $50 to have the phone replaced even with the protection plan. Another week passed, still no phone in the mail. We call again. They had a glitch and didn't send the phone. They said they would send it Monday. Now we're another week out. Going on 3 weeks with no phone and you better believe they're still pulling my full monthly payment out of my account 'on time' for a phone I do not even have. So between 360 monthly charge and the deductible I've paid for the full price of the phone anyway, 3 weeks later still do not have a phone and it's like trying to get a live person at the IRS to talk to someone. Also, even if you add another person as an authorized user they can't do any of this stuff. Only the main account holder can. We have a family of 4 on this, but no one but the main account holder can call and get anything done. They have terrible customer service and all their automation runs you in circles and makes it near impossible to get ahold of a live person. When you do they don't follow through. Going on week 4 with no phone for my son.

I would give 0 stars if I could. My grandmother had her device stolen. The store turned us away said we had to call assurance. She got the replacement phone the next day which was helpful and convenient. But that’s where it ended. She had to call to activate the SIM. Fine. But she is 73 and did not remember her pin. T mobile had her on hold for over an hour. When she said she didn’t know the pin they said they would text her something to confirm it was her. Now she is the ONLY phone on the plan. If she is calling to activate her phone how would she access the text? Idio-ts. Then the T-Mobile app says she does not have an ID. We go to create one and it says she already has one. Now she has to return to the store again. I will be shocked if they actually help her. She wanted to get a Metro PCS phone and I told her no use your insurance. So thank you T-Mobile for wasting our time and no you are NOT customer focused.

My phone of less than 6 months had an issue I called customer service, they sent me a used phone, it had the same issue, then they sent me another one, it didn't work either. So they promised me a free upgrade, that never materialized, I had to call to ask about it at least 5 times at which time they told me I would have to pay for it or I can have another used phone. They also promised I would not have to pay  for the used phones but they charged me anyway. This all took at least 10 calls and many hours of my time, for nothing!

So i ended my family plan for tmobile and paid all the fees and the account was disabled.. a month later i get a bill for $80 so i called tmobile and this guy was so disrespectful and didn’t have a valid reason for the bill nor wanted to transfer me to a supervisor and decided to leave me on hold for 5 minutes and hung up 

i then called for a second time and the lady seemed nice and promised a call back within 48 hours for the higher-ups to respond and now its been 7 days and she obviously didn’t call back..

I called a third time and told them about what the other two customer service agents did and this woman was apologizing and saying shes an expert and said she’ll contact these people and contact her supervisor and call me back after 48 hours (this was 2 days ago) so i was expecting a call today and, you know what, tmobile customer service dont care about clients satisfaction i assume and they just go there to have fun and get paid (i literally heard them talking and laughing on phone the third time i called)

edits.text: today i was on the line with tmobile customer service to resolve an issue but the person consistently repeated what i have been saying and so i requested to speak to a supervisor and they refused and after almost 20 minutes on the line and the person didn’t know what to do, they tried to internationally lose me by disrupting the connection and they were laughing and ended the call without me knowing..

text:
I switched to Tmobile 4 and a half years ago after being with Verizon for decades.  It was a scary move after being with a company for so long and mobile was pretty much just getting on a roll in the US.  I made the move and over all was happy with their service although the rural areas are a bit spotty to this day.  I live just outside the Bay Area and have to have antenna boosters in my house to get any service at all.  Now they are posting about being the best 5G around and how great it is and it makes you want to be part of the action but there is one problem, they want 4 grand to join in on the fun.  I can go get free 5G phones from any other major carrier but if I want to have the privilege to stay with Tmobile I will have to keep my old 4G Apple phones or dish out another 4 grand.  Hmm, let me think, should I fork out 4k or should I just change to another reputable carrier.  Well I know what I am going to do, the only question is whether to switch to Verizon or ATT.  I like Verizons coverage but I like the way ATT has customer loyalty.  All their new promotions say new or existing customers.  As a consumer I like that!

All and all I really liked doing business with Tmobile.  They remind me of the old southwest airlines with amazing customer service.  Unfortunately they are not a customer loyalty company and unfortunately I will not remain loyal to them.  Sometimes its better to invest in what you have versos what you want  and this is one company that has not figured that out.  Sad thing is I can go with to Verizon and get free iPhone 12's for the whole family, keep them and in 90 days Tmobile will buy me out of the contract at verizon.  I would not go backwards like that but how silly is it that they will do that to get a new customer but not retain what they already had!!  Really sad they have not figured this part out because they are a great

edits.text: I switched from Verizon to T-Mobile about 4 or 5 years ago.  The service has been so so, depends on where you are.  I have no reception at all at my house (1 bar and drops every call) and I am in a town of 100,000 in the bay area.   Over all I could deal with the sketchy service but anytime they come out with a new promotion it is geared only toward new customers.  So let's forget about the current customers that keep the lights on every month and pay the employee salaries and  let's put all our promo money into new customers instead that may or may not stay!!  Brilliant marketing plan!!   So we needed new phones because we have the Apple 7 phones that only have 16 GB of memory and with all the Apple update there is not enough room on the phone.  So I called customer service to see if there was anything  they could do to get us two new Apple XR phones (2 out of our 4 lines) and they talked about special programs that we could get like 400.00 as trade ins.  I said thats all you can do, they said yes.  So we started to look around at other carriers and happen to be at the mall and we said to ourselves that if they gave us 400 bucks for each of our phones that would be half off a new phone.  Not as good as being a new customer and getting a free phone but would be less hassle, no start up fees  with a new customer sign ups etc.  So we went into a T-Mobile store and they offered us like 100.00 for each phone, WHAT A JOKE.  One hand doesn't know what the other hand is doing.  The two people helping us had no knowledge of the new iPhone nor what  happens when you change plans etc because we have one free line so we just wanted to know if we change plans would we still get the free line or not.  One person said yes the other said no, NICE, we have no idea.  So we went ahead and spent like 850.00 on a new iPhone which they were giving to new customers because we figured we own it and we could just port our number when we change carriers.  The next day we received a survey to ask what we thought of or visit to the store so I answered it honestly.  About a week later  I get a call from T-Mobile customer service asking about my survey which I thought was great they cared enough to call!!  About a minute into the call it dropped due to bad service, doesn't look to good when you are on the phone with a cell phone provider and the call drops!  I figured they would call back, NEVER DID, NICE CUSTOMER SERVICE!!!  As of now we are just waiting for the best deal to come along for ATT or Verizon.  Save your money and your sanity, stick with ATT or Verizon

When my six year old misplaced a cell phone we called to suspend the service.  We simply use it for internet during long drives.  We called to suspend when he lost the phone only to find out that they will suspend the account but you still pay the full amount, WHAT A JOKE!!!  In order to not pay for it you must cancel the line so that you loose your phone number and if you find it and want to get a new number you have to pay the sign up fees again, GOOD BYE T-MOBILE  We are changing back to Verizon or going to AT&T  Service is spotty to begin with!!

text: Worst thing that ever happened—Sprint being taken over by TMobile!

T-Mobile has to be the worst scam company for cell phones. They give you a written agreement and in middle of the stream they change and then you have hundreds of dollars to pay at once. No Choice. This was not part of the original agreement. If you walk away from it, your Credit is affected. They make it sound so great and then they shaft you.

I would give - 10000 if I could  it is not the t mobile I had 6 years ago I went to atnt then i came back and everything has gone wrong giving  me bills to pay  that are not right i called 12 times in 1 month to fix bills it is stupid they lost their touch i would leave if it wasn't  a huge headache.

This is by far the worst service ever it was bad before but now that sprint and tmoble merged it became worse.... i never have service anywhere even in my home city and state horible experience with this company ever I cant wait to tell all my friends and family never to use this phone service I am also looking to find out if I can leave the service due to I signed with sprint not tmoble

Why do I give T Mobike a 0 starts review. 
Check this! 

1- I have a business account for over 2 years with T mobile with 2 lines on it! 

2- I pay for unlimited EVERYTHING on both lines. 

3. I run both of my main business via text (modern world and all you know, efficiency and what not) 

4. My service is suspended because I send multiple text messages in short amounts of time to multiple numbers/providers regarding our services, products, cars, etc. 

5. T mobile says its a PRIVILEGE to use their text messaging service...so they suspended it for 2 weeks based on SPAM policies. 

What is this elementary school detention or 9 yearld being grounded? 

6. It takes a team of "EXPERTS" almost a week to figure out "THE PROBLEM".
By a team as per T mobile
- 3 customer service reps
- 2 supervisors
- 2 ingeneering departments 

7. No attempt to mitigate the situation by offering a band-aid or SOMETHING. Literally **** you! Pay me attitude. 

For these 7 reasons I will be joining the other group of poor service providers at A&TT since thats about the only option I got other than rip.off Verizon. 
Also, going to IPhone again since they have a sweet deal on the 11.

Liars!!!!!! They keep changing their words and are leading your into scams and they’re fooling their customers. They keep charging us a lot of money even though we didn’t use any of what they charged us of. Manipulative people in customer service and in stores. All of them are manipulative.
I have no problems with calling but their internet connection is sometimes slow.

If I could give a half a star I would. We were with Sprint for 14 years. Once T Mobile bought out Sprint our service only works 40 percent of the time. And when you call customer service they say you have perfect service in your area. Well I can't make calls in my home, I cant even use my phone at work and it doesn't work while in my car.

 It's not the phones because we just got all new phones before T Mobile bought Sprint. And the phones worked great with Sprint.

 I would never recommend T Mobile to anyone. They service terrible.
T-Mobile sucks. 3 different calls to support and issue is still not fixed. Don't waste your time

Zero stars!!!!!!   Had metro and it wouldn’t work at my house told t-mobile I needed reliable service to switch to them (didn’t know they were the same company). Was guaranteed service and told would get promo and if autopay would only be $86 a month. First bill $130!!! Representative talked in circles and said couldn’t give refund of auto pay and next month would be $86. Told them phone service continued to cut out when in most areas and I was told it was due to sprint/t-mobile merge. Next month auto drafted $120 finally cancelled service and after cancellation auto drafted out $120 again!!!! Called customer service and was given run around. They apologized for bad service and misrepresentation but wouldn’t refund me. Crappy service- crappy customer service (can’t believe that I finally got an English as first language customer service rep) Steal your money- lie for sales- don’t care about their customers as long as they can make money! Beware and steer clear on T-mobile!!!!!!! My sprint worked great (was on plan with boss and she changed so I had to) funny t-mobile bought out sprint and that the service should have been a lot better with double the towers but it wouldn’t work at all!!!
Garbage garbage garbage. If these 3 words don’t stop anyone from getting T-Mobile u will regret if u go with T-Mobile. Service is garbage!!!
THEY KILLED SPRINT
i wish it could be zero,do yourself a favor and run away from these people,they hire children who have no respect and their customer service is the worst.

Very bad customer service.
Very very bad.

I am in Fort Collins Colorado. I had a very bad experience with the shift from sprint to T-mobile. Literally have been in the store for hours, probably a total of 15-20 hours over the past couple months to get my small business account cleared up. Ali was the worker who helped me through it and did an excellent job! Some of the best and most diligent customer service I have had. Sal is the manager there and seemed to be helpful as well. My experience with T-mobile has been a difficult one, and if it wasn't for the customer service at the Fort Collins store I would have taken my business account elsewhere. So kudos to Ali for helping us through this
Been a customer for over 5 years, this is a terrible company, I will be canceling with them in a few day, they charge you left and right, stay away from them

I have been a T-Mobile customer since 2007. I don't know if all of their recent acquisitions (Metro PCS and Sprint) is just too much infrastructure for the company to manage but the quality of service in my area has been on a steep decline since 2019. It has been so bad recently that I had to find a wi-fi connection just to connect with T-Mobile over their app. I'm in Houston, Texas so this isn't some rural area where there is only 1 pole in town. I have been in constant contact with T-Mobile over the last year trying to get them to understand the problem but they have been completely unhelpful. My phone can show full bars in 4G or 5G but my connection speed is well below 1 Mbps. That tells me that I've got good good signal strength but almost no throughput. I'm on the verge of switching to another provider because right now, all T-Mobile is providing is a monthly bill and NO service.

Buyer’s beware... these jacks strung me along just long enough for the return policy run out.. Then they upped my bill by twenty dollars and said the guy at the store miss quoted me and that they could not honor his mistake... bs this is a bait and switch scam to make money so they get you sucked in and then when they have you because it would cost you to much cancel... Then stick you with the extra money added to your bill!!

Associate Zelliece was professional, pleasant, and helpful. 

There were 10 employees and four customers which might explain why there were two additional associates hovering and interjecting questions, ideas, and suggestions.  It was a bit overwhelming. One salesperson in particular was extremely overbearing and pushy, suggesting phone upgrades for which I previously said I was not interested, and repeatedly suggesting a password reset. 

When I said for the fourth time that I was not interested in resetting my password, she became offended and walked out of the store. Let an associate do his/her job. Often customers complain that they have not received enough customers, too much service is just as bad, if not worse. Good customer service is really becoming hard to find. There are some simple expectations. 

1. Greet customers
2. Introduce yourself
3. Ask “how may I help you” or “what brings you in today?”
4. One associate should help a customers. If an additional associate is needed, introduce him/her. 
5. Offer information/options without bias
6. After a customer has said that he/she is not interested in an option, do not continue to bring it up unless the customer chooses to revisit the issue. 
7. Thank the customer. 
8. Let the customer know about other ways he/she seek assistance in the future (business card, website, or app)

Let start by saying this:do not switch to T-Mobile. Don’t let those “great deals hook you”. They are awful with customer service, their employees don’t know what the heck they are doing and as a consequence you will experience a nightmare. That was my experience with T-Mobile. I have been with Metro for 10years and saw a very good offer by T-Mobile on the iPhone 11 buy one get the second free by switching to T-Mobile. So I thought that’s a good deal. I call them and ask them ask all the questions I did have and finally I made the decision to take the deal. That’s when the nightmare started. To begin with they will start charging you since the day you opened the account even if you’re not on their network yet. Then to switch your line or lines in my case they will have you on the phone for ever then so I stoped half way through the day I was supposed to switch. I cancelled the plan and went back to metro PCs. Then they tried to charge me for the two phone after they have received the retuned items so yeah they are a pretty bad nightmare. Don’t doe it’s bad.

I have been a T-Mobile customer for about fifteen years. I purchased a new phone five days ago and got EXCELLENT service from Joseph and Belle expert reps. I received my phone the next day with free shipping. Walked into T-Mobile at the wellington green mall (Florida) and ALEX went above and beyond to get my new phone working, downloading apps for me because my old phone crashed so I could not transfer my information to the new phone. TMOBILE is my carrier.

DO NOT DO BUSINESS WITH THIS COMPANY THEY ARE THE WORST PHONE COMPANY EVER!!!! 
I MOVED TO MONTANA AND WAS TOLD BY THERE REP THAT THEY HAD THE BEST COVERAGE OUT OF ALL CARRIERS. WELL I HAD KEPT MY SPRINT PHONE ACTIVE AND DID A SIDE BY SIDE TEST MY SPRINT SERICE WAS WAY BETTER. SO I RETURNED THE 1500 DOLLAR PHONE 4 DAYS LATER BECAUSE THE SERVICE WAS SO BAD. I HAD TO DRIVE AN HOUR AND A HALF BACK TO THE STORE TO RETURN THE PHONE THEN WAS TREATED RUDELY BECAUSE I DIDNT GET RECEPTION REALLY!! OH YEAH AND CHARGED A 70 DOLLAR RESTOCKING FEE. WELL IT GETS BETTER NOW THEY CONTINUE TO BILL ME 88.00 FOR A SERVICE I CANCELLED 4 DAYS LATER. I AM ON THE SECOND ROBO CALL NOW WAITING TO SPEAK WITH A HUMAN. THE LAST CALL I WAS ON HOLD FOR 15 MINS AND FINALLY SOME PICK UP I COULD HEAR THEM BREATHING THEN THEY HUNG UP. THE WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EVER. IF YOU CALL THE CUSTOMER SUPPORT LINE IT TELLS YOU TO CALL BACK AFTER 6 AM THE NEXT DAY AND YOU GET THE SAME AT 6 AM. THESE ARE HORRIBLE PEOPLE STAY AWAY FROM THIS COMPANY. SORRY ABOUT THE CAPS BUT IM YELLING!!!!!!  I HAVE BEEN WITH SPRINT FOR 22 YEARS AND THEY WERE GREAT TO BAD THEY WILL GET RUINED BY THE MERGE WITH THIS CRAP COMPANY. BY THE WAY IM STILL ON HOLD WITH THEM TRYING TO COLLECT THIS DEBT.    I WAS JUST HUNG UP ON AFTER BEING ON HOLD FOR 22 MINS AFTER THEY CALLED ME !! YOU CANT EVEN MAKE THIS SH@# UP.

I’ve had T-Mobile for 8yrs never had much of a problem till this year! I paid my iPhone off 2yrs ago and had it for 4yrs I met all my requirements to the phone paid ON TIME! And paid my phone off! Which after 80 days when the phone paid off it should be unlocked. I switched service with 6 other phones on the plan all but 1 (which is mine) T-Mobile kept on lock and come to find out from Apple T-Mobile never sent any of the three requests to unlock my phone when I tried to put in my new service SIM card. This wasn’t a 3rd party phone I bought the phone from T-Mobile and now it’s been 12 days after I cut the service which I made sure we are DONE they don’t want to unlock my phone which is totally INSANE customer service is 100% trash and don’t CARE bout anyone! I hope everyone sees this and T-Mobile CEO see this cause this for real pissed me off!!

The service from T Mobile is terrible in my area and they have been difficult to contact and unhelpful in resolving the issue. They keep stringing me along with false solutions and I can't get out of contract without penalties.

Tmobile is worthless. That's being as kind as I can be. My 2 month old phone stopped charging, their customer service team is incapable of assisting without me traveling to a store.. The coverage is also not nearly as good as their maps suggest.
Great experience with the Patchogue store on Sunrise Highway. Wonderful people especially Vic. Honest talk no problems. Highly recommend.

Tmobile executive office runs its operations solely based on their ego. I had an active connection with tmobile. One day I lodged a complaint against tmobile, it was a genuine complaint, I sent the complaint directly to their top executives in writing. Following which the executive office of tmobile blocked my access to my account. Tmobile and it’s top executives are corrupted and lacks integrity. The executive office of Tmobile abused its powers by blocking my access to my phone account. Corruption to the max...

If you are reading this please do not purchase anything from T-Mobile not even a phone case. I ended service with them back in July 2020. I was told I needed to pay for the phones in order to cancel the service and it had to be before the next bill cycle I DID JUST THAT. I received a bill of $276.15 for the month of Sep-Oct. I called 10-23-20 three times 8:25am (30min call hung up on) 9:03am (16min call hung up on) 9:19am (18min) S/w a supervisor she told me the guy who ended my line only ended one line and suspend the other two (Who felt is that?) NOT MINE! So the lady told me she was sorry for the miss understanding and she will reimburse me my money back and also let they finance team know I am in good standards. So a few days go by and I received a letter from Collection. So I called back today 10-30-2020 s/w Jessica who told me all the last supervisor I s/w did was end the other two lines.
So I asked to s/w another supervisor she sent me to supervisor Janiece. Who was no help at ALLLLLL!! All she kept saying is I don't see where you cancel the service. I asked her do y'all have a finance team y'all work with? She told me NO. So everything that the last supervisor had told me was a lie just to get me off the phone and not to help me WOW!!!! I asked to s/w someone over her she told me it will be 24hrs until somebody reach out to me. I thought I was DONE with T-Mobile back in July. This is not my felt that T-MOBILE PICK A TEAM WHO DO NOT KNOW HOW TO DOCUMENT. This company does not understand quality because of the way my case is handle. IF ANYBODY IS READING THIS THAT WORK FOR T-MOBILE TELL THE BIG BOSS TO CALL ME ASAP!!!! Account # 967445093

I am a long time sprint customer, I recently had a phone replaced after breaking the camera on my s20 ultra 5g. I allowed the store employee to talk me into taking the t mobile experience. He inserted the t mobile card into my new phone and I have had nothing but problems since, no service,no wifi calling. After contacting tech support multiple times, driving an hour and a half one way to the t mobile store to fix my issue as instructed by tech support only to be told they would be closing soon and they couldn't help me. I was there a half hour before close! I tried again this morning with sprint tech support! Still no fix and only getting the run around! If this is the t mobile experience, I'm switching to Verizon!

I had the greatest experience at T-mobike since I've been a customer. I visited the Winder,Ga store on May street. Your agent in this store Clara was the friendliest and most helpful person I've ever encountered. She took care of all my needs in an exceptional manner. She is super.

The merger with sprint was horrible. I have been a Sprint customer for over 5 years and have always been able to upgrade my phone. Now, T-Moible requires all of the money upfront. Even during a pandemic this company seems to only care about $. I have been on the phone with customer service for over two hours and yet I just keep getting transferred from one person to another reiterating the same dilemma. I never experienced thislevel of frustration with Sprint.

Worst UX design and customer service of all time! I went into the website of "My T-Mobile" and wanted to check my plan to see if I could add Mexico to my coverage for a month. When I go to look at my plan I get a blank page with a phone number and no option to even see my plan. Then I call the number on the page and get am forced to listen to a buck of ads then finally get past the adds and a computer tells me there is no t-mobile at this number and that I must call a different number. That number goes to AT&T where another computer makes me give information and then says I don't exist in their system....Ok, so I go back into the site and literally have no option of seeing any of my information. I can't see my plan much less make any changes to it.

Been w tmobile for many years now. Over the past 4 or 5 months their service has become totally unreliable. Dont fall for their hype. Paying 70 a month for only 2 gb of data. I have called in many times to ask why their service is now so crappy. They give me run around every time they tell me I am "grandfathered in" and I am receiving the best deal w them ! Lol Now I am finally switching. Don't fall for their bs excuses. I am get 3 gb for only 30 bucks a month with my new carrier. Bye bye tmobile! Also everyone else i speak is having same issues. What a joke!

I originally went to T-Mobile (Taylor,Tx) for information so I could compare companies. Scott, Joanna & Juan were AMAZING!! They were very knowledgeable,  understanding & helpful,  explaining everything & answering all my questions.  I gave them my budget and I walked out with 2 awesome phones, a perfect plan suited to fit my needs and I even got some little gifts for T-Mobile Tuesday. Thank you thank you thank you! I highly recommend T-Mobile !

Very poor coverage.   I get almost no signal in my office even though I sit next to a window and have upgraded my phone to the latest 5G phone.   And I just completed a trip from MN to AZ and found that the coverage was so bad that the Google maps did not work along the interstate or around town in Tucson.   I could not even send text messages from the hotel I was at in Tucson.   If you want a phone that works, then avoid t-mobile.

T-Mobile customer for about 3.5 years. 17 months ago I added a line and a phone for my kid. My T-Mo bill is paid automatically, and I don't usually review the bill online. But I did today and found a $15/mo. service ("ONE Plus") I never requested and have no use for. Called T-Mo to ask why it was there. Evidently, the service was magically added without my knowledge or authorization when I obtained the 2nd line & phone. The rep today said, "We're not supposed to refund more than 3-6 months, but..." T-Mo is refunding 18 months of unauthorized charge ($270.00) to my account. If you find bogus charges on your bill, do not accept less than FULL credit for all such charges.

As of now I've only been with t-mobile since September 10 2020 I got two phones and one is a apple the other one is A   Android that I have had problems with since day one dropped calls and internet only working half the time texts and drop calls or not receiving calls made to me or they go to voice mail or it don't answer at all I've called in about ten times they have hung up on me or tell me one thing do a other I wanting out of the contract with the phones and go to a different phone company I have credit on my account but they allow me to use it for another phone only bill that they messed up this company is a big joke

Worst customer service ever. Close the account to go to Verizon and they send my account to collections in 1 week. After that i settle the account with the agency. Amsher collection services. Now they said i owe them money and they wont unlock my phone. Even when i gave them transaction number,date, name of person evey single piece of information still said i owe them money. Worst company ever. Tmobile steal your money.
TMobile is a bad Company 8 years with them and get treated very Bad I will go with a another company t Mobile is trash. Don’t go with t mobile

For anyone considering getting service from this carrier please read. I signed up for service with this company after reviewing their coverage map and confirming coverage in my area. I was really excited to get such an affordable plan and new iPhones. I received my new phones and activated the service, turns out the service in my area was not good and I barely had any, calls were dropped and I was lucky to get 1 bar. I reviewed their coverage map again and found in the fine print that coverage in my area was best if I’m outside. I live in MN, it’s freezing here in the winter. I called the customer service line on 7/28/20 and told them I wanted to cancel service per the 14 day cancellation and return agreement. I could barely hear the person I spoke to because the service was so bad. He tried to tell me that I would be responsible for the full bill in August even though I couldn’t even use the phone’s because the service was that bad. I said your coverage maps are misleading and that I would pay $20 for the last 2 weeks in July that were in the billing cycle and he agreed. I sent in the 3 phones I got with the service and received a refund for the activation fee. Fast forward to September, I see a charge on my credit card. I started a chat convo and told them that I canceled the account on 7/28 and they debited my card in error. They were sorry, blah, blah and said they would cancel the account and refund my card. Fast forward to October and you guessed it they charged my card again. I again chatted with them on 10/12/20 and at this point I’m pretty ticked because I don’t have money to spare during this pandemic and again they are sorry, blah blah, agree to refund me $280 plus the $20 I paid for July’s service. They said the account would be canceled and I would get the refund in 3 days (I have a screenshot of the chat convo for this) it’s now 10/25/20 and I still don’t have a refund. I attempt to chat and they tell me to call so now I’m pissed because I want this in writing because the first time I tried to cancel the account I called and they never canceled it and I don’t have proof. So what can I do other than call? I spoke to a guy named Paul that said he saw the account was canceled and a refund should be issued on 11/11/20 but puts me on hold to speak to his boss. He comes back on the phone and tells me I’ll receive a gift card 30 days from now for $280, not $300 which is what I was originally told. (See pic). Now I’m 10x more upset bc this dude is super condescending and I asked why I wouldn’t get the $ put back on my card and he gives me some BS so I tell him I called on 7/28 to get the account canceled to which he says that I only ported the numbers and didn’t cancel the account at that time (even though I already received a refund for the activation fee) I have screenshots of all the convos but of course when it gets into fraudulent charges territory you have to call and have no proof and considering how this has went down I’m not too confident in their recordings being released without legal action.
Please keep this in mind if you decide to switch to T-Mobile. Thanks for reading.
On hold for 28 min listening to terrible pop music while trying automated menu tells me repeatedly “Team Mobile customers are the happiest....” . If that’s your kind of happy, go for it.

My husband's phone is a T-mobile so when he couldn't connnect I tried calling customer service.  After being on hold for 30 minutes and called back.  There is an option to have them call you so I tried that and never received a call.  I tried again the next morning and again no call back.  So I waited on hold until I finally got someone in customer service.  Unfortunately, English wasn't her first language and we had communication problems.  During this whole mess they called my husband and he received the call so I figured we were good.  No.  I refused to call T-mobile again so we took his phone to a T-mobile store and got the help we needed there.  This place has the WORST customer service I've ever dealt with!

very regret it changing to T-mibile :(

I started the Jump program on 9/04/2020 and since then I have had nothing but trouble. They sent my new phone through the mail, I received it and I sent the old phone into their warehouse. I received an email saying they did not have my phone so I started dealing with customer service and got the run around for a month telling me that they could not find the phone I sent in. Then after dealing with them for a month I called the corporate office in Bellevue Washington and spoke to a man named Gabe. I told him everything that had been going on and that the only thing I wanted to do was to take the new phone in and close my account after being told by one of their experts that I had sent in an empty box. Well I was not on the phone with him more than 5 minutes and guess what they found the phone. I was still not happy since I had been told i had sent in an empty box. So I told him again that all I wanted to do  was take the phone back into them and close my account and he started in on that I had been with them for so long and that I should not let 1 little incident take me away from the company and I told him I was still not happy and he said that he would give me a $50.00 credit to my account. They can keep the $50.00, he may call it one little incident but being told that I had sent in an empty box and pretty much told I was lying about sending it in now mind you he never called me a liar but he sure pointed to it several times. So a small piece of advice don't use t mobile or sprint unless you want to be screwed over and over it took 11 calls and me giving them the tracking information 11 times before their ever found the phone and then they only found it after I called corporate so something does not sound right. You may pay a little more with another company but it will be worth not being told that things being said is only 1 little incident and thinking $50.00 dollars is going to fix everything, guess what it hasn't I am going on every website that I can find and leave a review about how I was treated by their team of experts and then how corporate treated me. I will also be filing a repos.t with the Washington State AG office on Monday morning. So just a little piece of advice unless you like being treated badly I would go elsewhere to look for a phone and stay completely away from T Mobile and Sprint they are both owned by the same company.

I have been with tmobile for 8 years. They have a habit of adding fees and services to your account even when you didn't ask for it. For example, they asked me if I wanted insurance on one of the phones I bought, I specifically said no. NO. Not yes I said NO. Months later I go through my bill and find out they have been charging me for insurance on the phone that I specifically told them NO. To add to that, I added my sister and mom to my line and I put stronger hotspot on my mom's phone (15 dollars extra a month) and when they asked if we wanted it on my sisters phone we said NO. A year later (I am not kidding ) I was looking in depth at my charges and come to find out they have been charging me the extra 15 dollars not only on my mom's but on my sisters line which we specifically said NO to. I complained and complained and all they were able to do was refund me three months. Three months out of a whole freaking years worth of money on something that I NEVER agreed to. They are exhausting. I don't want to have to go in depth every single month into my bill to see if they are charging me extra. It's so so so annoying. I have not one single clue why I am still with them. If I didn't have my entire family on my plan I would leave them in a heartbeat . Seriously I advise you to go elsewhere.

GALAXY A71 5G -This phone has so many connection issues. I reviewed this phone for weeks before purchasing. I get it and start having specific issues with connection , dropping calls, search issues etc. only to find out that I was looking in the wrong place for my reviews. DO NOT PURCHASE THIS PHONE. There is a software issue with this phone that samsung IS aware of and has not put out an update to fix it. You will call in and someone will tell you to factory reset your phone , they will say they have not heard of any issues. Let me tell you, if you look it up under connection issues you will see all types of review. All the major carriers push this phone knowing it has issues which is wrong...shame on you! and shame on samsung for not fixing the issues. I've always loved the Galaxy phones, cheaped out on this model to save a few bucks, sure wish I had not. Now, i'm stuck with a 500.00 piece of junk!!!!

We went with T Mobile, for internet - after years of terrible problems with Orange. (Another AWFUL company, only interested in money) - anyway, we were promised the earth from T mobile, fastest speeds, best connection, most masts closest to our house, etc. And we got that - for the first two months of our contract. Brilliant speeds. Now we are down to dial up speeds. Gell away to absolutely nothing, And the clown on the phone is telling us because there are a lot of people online! (After two months) - With no help from T-Mobile whatsoever, other than an insulting waffle from a brain dead telephone rep of the company who has less of a clue about the internet than my local shopkeeper. If we do not get speed back on our router, like we had it, T-Mobile can stick their bloody contract where the sun don't shine. Feeling deeply conned.

UPDATE: 

T-Mobile are thieves. Like most, if not all, communications companies, but T-Mobile are really manipulative. This is how they work. They call you, offering the fastest speed possible. They give you a trial, and it IS the fastest of speed. You will delight at the speed of the trial, but what they don't tell you is that once you sign the contract, they will throttle the speed by 90% if you use the internet regularly. "Fair use policy" they call it. But there is no "fair use" policy on their trial, meaning, they tempt you to sign a two year contract by giving you super fast internet speed, then, once you sign the contract, and are expecting the same service, they point you to the hidden little text in the contract, which states they have right to cut your internet speed by 90%. T-Mobile are nothing other than snake oil selling con men, which the world would be better without. If you want a fast interent speed, take their trial, then ditch them, before they cut your internet speed by 90%.

I am a former T-Mobile customer that feels very disappointed and enraged at the same time. I had been with T-Mobile for many years honestly because it was more affordable. Throughout this time there were many times where I would get frustrated with the poor cellular service I would receive at many times everywhere (this is common knowledge though), sometimes even inside my own home. I contemplated leaving many times but I couldn’t afford it at that time. The audacity they have to heavily advertise as being a company that offers cell phone service in Mexico in Canada is completely comical and a flat out plain lie. I go to Mexico quite often and never got service there. I couldn’t make phone calls, no internet, texts....I spend a lot of time in Alaska, and actually spent 4 months quarantined there this year and apparently used up domestic roaming to the point that my line got disconnected. When I tried to call and get my line connected again they wanted to rerun my credit to restart my line. Absolutely not! Alaska is the 49th state in the USA and when I asked them why would I be “domestic roaming” at all they said T-Mobile doesn’t have towers in Alaska. So wait, you offer “cell phone service” in Mexico and Canada but not in one of your own 50 states?! BS! The supervisor didn’t even try to excuse or defend them as a company why should I stay as a customer? I didn’t. I still feel frustrated though. They answer their phone calls saying they offer 10 star service. Now that was truly laughable and more lies...This company is a joke but I get why people stay it is more affordable..
Do not use T-Mobile. They arbitrarily put insurance on 2 phones that was never authorized. Refuse to reimburse the $1370 owed. They are a scam.

We're small business owners that operate from a home office.  We live in the Foothills West of Denver and had great service with Sprint.  T Mobile bought Sprint and immediately our cell phones no longer work.  Same with the Sprint hot spot MiFi.  We've spent more than 2 days trying to rectify the signal loss both on the phone and in the T Mobile stores to no avail.  The coverage map that T Mobile supplies is an absolute lie.  The nearest spot we can now get a signal is 5 miles from our home.  And, yes, we're cancelling our service since it does us no good whatsoever.

When I first got T-Mobile, it was great! Had a new phone, new line for a decent price. Everything went downhill when they merged with sprint. Service was awful- I lived in a city. They didn’t tell me that they would cut my service off immediately and I would have to wait 72 hours to get an email that would inform me that my iPhone was unlocked from T-Mobile even after I paid a whole last months Bill and an extra 250 dollars to pay off my phone.  That’s a safety hazard. I am on hour 25 with no service- only being able to be on wifi because my iPhone still isn’t unlocked. I found my way to a T-Mobile somehow and they just shrugged and said “sorry we can’t do anything. Almost like a punishment. So anyway, T-Mobile/sprint has gone downhill so drastically so much to the point that they’ll even punish you once you leave.

I switched to tmobile because I was getting awful coverage from my provider. Tmobile was running a promo up to $600 back to put toward your old carriers final bill. Where to start.... 3 years ago someone pretending to be me called t mobile and cancelled my business line. 10 minutes later i contacted t mobile and they could not get my phone number back. It cost me thousands of dollars. I was not offered anything for my troubles. Less than 2 years ago an employee marked the wrong IMEI number for a warrantied phone. This resulted in my service being shut off multiple times a week for 10 months. After spending over 100 hours on the phone a single employee figured out the issue and took care of it. I was not offered anything for my troubles Now for the last 8 months my service has not worked. I never get calls. I cant make calls majority of the time. The internet, maps, music etc never work because of it. Ive been calling for a supervisor since February only to be offered supervisor call backs. Ive never once received a call back as promised, because they cant get through to me. Ive sent hundreds of screen shots that confirm my service issues. ive seen the local store manager who confirmed my cell line has issues. Ive had customers even write letters to tmobile about how they cannot get ahold of me. Finally since February I now have multiple employees advising me that there are service issues in my area. It took nearly 9 months to finally get a supervisor on the phone. Ive spent another 100+ hours on the phone thus past year. They have now not only not offered me nothing for my troubles, they are actually telling me instead of doing something to credit my account that i should just cancel my service. I have more than enough physical evidence to back claims. They refuse to be accountable. They refuse to put me on the phone with a supervisor. Instead if i ask for a supervisor they put me on hold and then hang up the call. This has been happening since February. I refuse to pay for a service that NEVER works. They refuse to actually do their jobs and fulfil their own service agreement. I have more than enough evidence.

Please don’t go to Tmobile.  I thought I was getting a good deal with five lines for 118.  I thought wow.... Until the bait and switch.  I was told the bill would be one thing after I paid 30 activation for each line, which came to 135.  I said ok activation, well it got worse.  I had 14 days to think through and the representative stated I would have 14 days to decide and I would get my money back if I decided not to stay.  On the second day of service I got another bill for 122 that was due within 20 days.   I had already shelled out 135 plus another 122 not gonna do it.  I cancelled on the 13th day and went to spectrum mobile which is a GOD sent and I love them.  BUT getting back to T-mobile, I was informed that I would not get my activation fee back even though I had cancelled during the remorse 14 day period.  I was told I would not get a bill but today I was informed I would get  another bill.  I said damn, I really hate this company.  To top it off, I had my mother and sister on my plan in another part of the state and they could not get service outside their home and that was another reason besides the bait and switch I moved to another carrier. PLEASE DO NOT GO TO TMOBILE THEY ARE RAGGEDY AND NOT TRUSTWORTHY.  DO NOT DO NOT GO TO THEM, YOU WILL BE SORRY!!!                     BAIT AND SWITCH COMPANY

Left At&t two years ago because At&t was too expensive. I was paying $300 a month on a family plan with five phones. Moved to T-Mobile and my bill was $195 a month. I had 4 issues all solved by US support people. International roaming is great. I have used once a year sometimes more.  No charges at all since T-Mobiles explains what is free and what is charged..   We got to test T-Mobile before switching we knew where my parents live there is only one bar of signal but as to wifi calling it did not matter compared to we live we get nearly full signal.   T-Mobile was very helpful to my parents as to the low signal they sent out extender boxes once in stock free which fixed issue from zero or one bars of signal to 2-3 bars inside.   Once the towers for Sprint are switched to T-mobile my parents will have more signal as Sprint has a tower nearby with out the need for the extender signal boxes.

Paying less, free global roaming, and US support no complaints.

Have been given the run around 3 times on an incorrect billing issue. I decided to switch my phone service to t mobile on their “bring your own device” plan. Whenever I was signing up for some reason it allowed me to get through the entire process and then said my phone was not eligible for that plan (iPhone 6s). Mind you since it let me get through the entire process that means they already had my card information, but wouldn’t allow me to use my current phone with their plan,so I decided to look elsewhere. About a day after getting the notice I was not eligible, I got an email saying my order was completed and they were shipping out my SIM card. I had already chosen a new company so I called them and canceled the plan, and informed them that it stated I was not eligible so I’m not sure why they sent me the SIM card and continued with the account. She apologized and cancelled the account, however about a week later my card was charged $85 from t mobile (not to mention $85 a month for one phone is absolutely ridiculous). I called customer service and informed her the account was cancelled yet I was still charged. She apologized and said it did show that it was cancelled and that I had never used the account and she explained that the previous woman that cancelled the account cancelled it incorrectly, so it was set to cancel after the first billing cycle. She said she issued a full refund and was sorry about the issue, and it would take 3 to 5 days for the refund to be issued. I waited a week and after receiving no refund I called customer service AGAIN, and this time the representative stated that the refund was declined and she was not sure why. She put me on hold and went to speak with a manager and said she got the issue resolved and I would be refunded the full amount in 3 to 5 days. I think we’re seeing a pattern here so you can definitely bet I did not receive that refund, and so I called again. This time I was told the refund was sent incorrectly and was refunded to the account instead of directly back to my card. WHY IN THE WORLD WOULD THE MONEY BE REFUNDED TO A CANCELLED ACCOUNT?! At this point I’m frustrated and feel as though I’m being lied to, however I’m not gonna be upset with the representative because each time it has been someone different so it would be unfair for me to throw the issue off on them, so instead my issue is with T-Mobile as a whole and their poorly trained staff. I’ll just go ahead and dispute the transaction with my bank, at least the bright side is I didn’t go with this company after all

NUMEROUS COMPLAINTS NUMEROUS TICKETS PLACED BY TECH SUPPORT AND STILL NOT RESOLVED.  PHONE 1+8
DOESN'T WORK WHEN YOU ARE PLACING A CALL TO ANOTHER 1+8 PHONE.

Joined TMobile in July to take advantage of the 1+8 phone at 50 percent off.  I purchase 2 of the 1+8.  One for me and one for my husband.  Ever since I joined in July calls between both of our phones only work 25 percent of the time.  Calls to other people is ok since they have other type of phones. Problem is calling each other on the same brand and model phone 1+8.  I've been complaining every few weeks and Tech Support has been writing "tickets" but the problem is still there.  Technical support keeps saying there's an issue with the software of this 1+8 phone and they are working on it with the manufacturer.  I've been hearing the same excuse for 3 months.
You hear a silence for 30 seconds then go to voicemail.  Other times one of us cannot hear the other when by a miracle the phone rings and goes through.  We end up having to text.
Check the history on BOTH phones and you'll see the same number dialed repeatedly because we are unable to establish communication.
We are in our 70's and I'm staying at my daughter's because I had surgery 2 weeks ago.he's also 70 and is incommunicated at our house not being able to call us in case of an emergency.
TMobile deserves a rating of -0
Now you are replacing both phones with the same brand as the one we already have and DON'T WORK when you try to make a call to the same type of phone.
RIDICULOUS!!!

My Galaxy S7 Edge lost the Messages icon on the screen. I called 611 and spoke by phone with an expert. He was unable to fix the problem. I went today to the T Mobile store at 10259 S. Post Oak in Houston. I was very satisfied with the team. I was assisted by Monica who was very motivated to solve my problem. She spent lots of time trying to figure out the resolution. Soon other team members assisted her, including Yuri. They all did google searches, brainstormed, and refused to give up. Monica and her support team solved my problem. I left feeling great about their respect of Covid, their fund of knowledge, and their desire to resolve my problem.
